summary: First-iteration full E2E test for SINKRONIZE. Backend: 27 pytest tests covering apps, auth, affiliations, checkout split, reviews, wallet, materials, notifications, stats. Found and fixed 1 root-cause bug (MongoDB ObjectId in 4 POST responses). Frontend: smoke-tested landing, marketplace, login and producer dashboard via Playwright - all rendered with real seeded data.

issues: Some Unsplash hero/trust-logos appear as plain text 'loft / ifood / stone / creditas...' rather than brand logos - cosmetic only

critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 680 lines mixing models, helpers, auth, apps, sales, stats - consider splitting into routers/ when adding new features

Several endpoints previously returned the same dict passed to insert_one() which Motor mutates with ObjectId - bug fixed in /auth/register, /affiliations/{app_id}, /checkout, /reviews, /wallet/withdraw by popping _id after insert; recommend adopting pattern: build doc, insert, then return projection from DB or strip _id explicitly to prevent regressions
Producer dashboard chart x-axis labels read '09/05'..'22/05' (DD/MM) but only the date '22/05' has data because dates use timezone.utc - acceptable but mention timezone handling later
POST /api/auth/session swallows Emergent token even if create_session would create a duplicate entry; consider idempotent upsert
test_credentials: producer@sinkronize.com / demo1234 ; afiliado@sinkronize.com / demo1234 (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md - seeded ok)
seed_data_creation: Seed runs on startup creating 9 apps + 2 demo users; verified via GET /api/apps and login
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend fully green. Pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py is regression-ready. Frontend deeper testing (register flow with role selection, affiliate dashboard /afiliado, wallet /carteira UI form, checkout /checkout/:slug submission, review submission UI, copy-affiliate-link toast) not exercised - main agent or next tester should verify these UI flows. Google OAuth flow was skipped per instructions.
rca of the issue: ROOT CAUSE (FIXED): pymongo/motor's insert_one() mutates the input dict by setting `_id: ObjectId(...)`. Five endpoints (/auth/register, /affiliations/{app_id}, /checkout, /reviews, /wallet/withdraw) returned that same dict directly, so FastAPI's jsonable_encoder hit 'TypeError: ObjectId is not iterable' -> 500. Mitigation applied: doc.pop('_id', None) immediately after each insert_one before returning. Verified by re-running pytest (4 failed -> 27 passed).
